name: Lean Manufacturing for Aerospace
summary: A production-improvement approach focused on reducing waiting, rework, transport, and excess inventory while preserving aerospace quality and traceability.
whenToUseIt: Use it in assembly, composites, and machining environments where cycle time is long and defects or handoffs are driving cost.
steps: Map the current value stream from raw material to delivered assembly.
Identify bottlenecks, queue time, rework loops, and information delays.
Standardise work and move defect detection earlier.
Rebalance the line for smoother flow and clearer visual control.
Track cycle time, first-pass yield, and on-time delivery after the change.
example: A composite panel cell may spend more time waiting for cure slots, inspection sign-off, and kitting than actually being built. Lean analysis exposes those delays and makes targeted improvements possible.
